title: uragoga segregata
intro: Uragoga segregata, also known as bitter apricot, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible fruit. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, attributed to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Uragoga segregata include its potential to aid in digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and reduce fever. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or dried forms, and its bitter compounds are also used in traditional remedies.
